root_cause.text: The loop parsing the first line of a mapped script to skip a hashbang sequence fails to check if the index exceeds the length of the memory-mapped buffer, allowing it to read past the end of the buffer if no newline character is found.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces the unbounded condition `c != EOF` with a strict bounds check `i < file_handle.handle.stream.mmap.len` inside the `while` loop, and adds a similar check before accessing `file_handle.handle.stream.mmap.buf[i]` when checking for a `\n` after a `\r`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The loop parsing the first line of a mapped script to skip a hashbang sequence fails to check if the index exceeds the length of the memory-mapped buffer, allowing it to read past the end of the buffer if no newline character is found.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces the unbounded condition `c != EOF` with a strict bounds check `i < file_handle.handle.stream.mmap.len` inside the `while` loop, and adds a similar check before accessing `file_handle.handle.stream.mmap.buf[i]` when checking for a `\n` after a `\r`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the NEWS file to document the fix for bug #68618 in php-cgi.
intent_record.what: Added a changelog entry for bug #68618 under the CGI section.
intent_record.how: Updates the NEWS file to inform users about a bug fix regarding an out of bounds read crash in php-cgi.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add bounds checking to memory-mapped buffer parsing to prevent out-of-bounds reads.
intent_record.what: Replaced EOF checks with buffer length boundary checks during character iteration and added an upper bound clamp for the index.
intent_record.how: Prevents out-of-bounds memory access by ensuring the iteration index does not exceed the memory-mapped buffer's length.

grouping_result.intent: Update the NEWS file to document the fix for bug #68618 in php-cgi.
Add bounds checking to memory-mapped buffer parsing to prevent out-of-bounds reads.

relevance_review.reason: The group only updates the NEWS file to document the bug fix and does not contain any code changes addressing the vulnerability.
The group's intent to add bounds checking to memory-mapped buffer parsing directly addresses the vulnerability's root cause, which is the lack of bounds checking leading to an out-of-bounds read.

candidates.rationale: The loop condition fails to check if the index 'i' exceeds the length of the memory-mapped buffer.
This statement reads from the memory-mapped buffer, potentially reading past its end due to the missing bounds check.
This check also reads from the buffer at index 'i' without verifying if 'i' is within bounds.

vuln_statements.explanation: This loop condition fails to check if the index 'i' is within the bounds of the memory-mapped buffer, leading to out-of-bounds reads.
This statement reads from the memory-mapped buffer, potentially reading past its end because the loop condition does not verify bounds.
This check accesses the buffer at index 'i' without verifying if 'i' is within the bounds of the buffer length.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Use zend_stream API to open main PHP script in CGI/FastCGI. This allows to override the open function by opcode caches a
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b4216e0a82f509532513a9a6bc1882b75918f341
Author: Dmitry Stogov <dmitry@php.net>
Date:   Wed Jul 7 16:54:55 2010 +0000

    Use zend_stream API to open main PHP script in CGI/FastCGI. This allows to override the open function by opcode caches and eliminate the actual open syscall.

diff --git a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
index 9b35003af4d..dd2a2e3b9f4 100644
--- a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
+++ b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
@@ -2079,23 +2079,75 @@ consult the installation file that came wi
bic_trace_step.reason: The newly added `ZEND_HANDLE_MAPPED` case introduces the vulnerable loop `while (c != '\n' && c != '\r' && c != EOF) { c = file_handle.handle.stream.mmap.buf[i++]; }`. This loop fails to check if the index `i` exceeds the length of the memory-mapped buffer, allowing an out-of-bounds read if no newline character is found.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: MFH: Improved and cleaned CGI code. FastCGI is now always enabled and can not be disabled. See sapi/cgi/CHANGES for more
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 06f43b30c1c50ae8cafd35dd2207e3bbda7c3e3c
Author: Dmitry Stogov <dmitry@php.net>
Date:   Thu Sep 27 08:23:58 2007 +0000

    MFH: Improved and cleaned CGI code. FastCGI is now always enabled and can not be disabled. See sapi/cgi/CHANGES for more details.

diff --git a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
index 601938aed4c..6af8da4e2b5 100644
--- a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
+++ b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
@@ -80,7 +80,6 @@ int __riscosify_control = __RISCOSIFY_STRICT_UNIX_SPECS;
 
 #include "php_g
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided code does not contain the vulnerable construct. The shebang parsing in `cgi_main.c` uses `fgetc` on a `FILE *` stream rather than a memory-mapped buffer index loop as described in the vulnerability root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Use zend_stream API to open main PHP script in CGI/FastCGI. This allows to override the open function by opcode caches a
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b4216e0a82f509532513a9a6bc1882b75918f341
Author: Dmitry Stogov <dmitry@php.net>
Date:   Wed Jul 7 16:54:55 2010 +0000

    Use zend_stream API to open main PHP script in CGI/FastCGI. This allows to override the open function by opcode caches and eliminate the actual open syscall.

diff --git a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
index 9b35003af4d..dd2a2e3b9f4 100644
--- a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
+++ b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
@@ -2079,23 +2079,75 @@ consult the installation file that came wi
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the ZEND_HANDLE_MAPPED case which contains a while loop that reads characters from the mmap.buf to skip the shebang line. It does not check if the index 'i' exceeds 'mmap.len', allowing an out-of-bounds read if no newline is found. This perfectly matches the root cause.
